context: Tests whether the agent applies the correct blur threshold (40, not 80) for pale-skin-safe capture validation, targets the correct intra-class distance range, uses pickle for enrollment persistence, and avoids re-enrolling from raw images in the load path.

checklist.name: Blur threshold is 40
checklist.description: The Laplacian variance rejection threshold used in frame validation is 40 (not 80, not 100, not 150)

checklist.name: Blur threshold not 80
checklist.description: Does NOT use 80 as the blur threshold (80 is explicitly the wrong value that causes false rejections for pale skin)

checklist.name: Intra-class mean target lower bound
checklist.description: Pre-publish validation checks or design_notes.md states intra-class distance mean target minimum of 0.25 (not lower)

checklist.name: Intra-class mean target upper bound
checklist.description: Pre-publish validation checks or design_notes.md states intra-class distance mean target maximum of 0.40 (not higher)

checklist.name: Intra-class max check
checklist.description: Pre-publish validation checks or design_notes.md flags intra-class max distance > 0.50 as an outlier signal

checklist.name: Pickle for enrollment storage
checklist.description: Enrollment is saved using pickle (not JSON, not numpy .npy, not CSV) — design_notes.md or code uses pickle.dump/pickle.load

checklist.name: No re-enrollment in load path
checklist.description: The production load path loads from the pickle file directly — it does NOT re-compute encodings from JPEG/image files at load time

checklist.name: Pale skin rationale
checklist.description: design_notes.md mentions that pale or fair skin produces lower Laplacian variance on sharp photos, justifying the lower blur threshold
